lede: A look at September 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Manuel Montt and Raúl Albiol, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Pope Benedict XVI
events.desc: Pope Benedict XVI becomes the longest-lived pope, 93 years, four months, 16 days, surpassing Pope Leo XIII, who died in 1903.

events.title: Al-Qaeda
events.desc: Three terrorists suspected to be a part of Al-Qaeda are arrested in Germany after allegedly planning attacks on both the Frankfurt International airport and US military installations.

events.title: Google
events.desc: Google is founded by Larry Page and Sergey Brin, two PhD students at Stanford University.

events.title: Leipzig
events.desc: In Leipzig, East Germany, the first of weekly demonstration for the legalisation of opposition groups and democratic reforms takes place.

events.title: Golden Dragon massacre
events.desc: The Golden Dragon massacre takes place in San Francisco.

events.title: Salvador Allende
events.desc: Salvador Allende is elected President of Chile.

events.title: San Francisco
events.desc: The first live transcontinental television broadcast takes place in San Francisco, United States, from the Japanese Peace Treaty Conference.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: William J. Murphy commands the first Royal Air Force attack on Germany.

events.title: Albania
events.desc: Albanian rebels succeed in their revolt when the Ottoman Empire agrees to fulfill their demands

events.title: Great Fire of London
events.desc: In London, England, the most destructive damage from the Great Fire occurs.

births.name: Manuel Montt
births.line: Chilean scholar and politician, 6th President of Chile

births.name: Dadabhai Naoroji
births.line: Indian academic and politician, President of the Indian National Congress

births.name: George Gray (chemist)
births.line: British chemist, developer of stable liquid crystals

deaths.text: E. F. Schumacher, German-English economist and statistician
Albert Schweitzer, French-Gabonese physician, theologian, and missionary, Nobel Prize laureate
Robert Schuman, Luxembourgian-French politician, 130th Prime Minister of France

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Catherine of Racconigi
Christian feast day: Moses and Aaron (Lutheran Church and Eastern Orthodox Church)
